steps.title: Interpret the function and statement
steps.content:
The function $f(x) = 240,000(1.22)^x$ outputs predicted annual revenue in dollars, where $x$ is years after the company started.

The notation $f(5)$ represents the revenue at year 5 — a snapshot value, not a growth factor.

The statement f(5) is approximately equal to 648,650 means this revenue is approximately \$648,650.

steps.title: Identify the correct choice
steps.content:
This directly matches the correct answer, A. We can eliminate the other choices:
  • "$5$ years after the company started selling jewelry online, its predicted annual revenue will have increased by a total of approximately $648,650$ dollars.": Refers to cumulative increase from start, not the actual revenue at year 5.
  • "When the company's predicted annual revenue is approximately $648,650$ dollars, it is $5$ times the predicted annual revenue for the previous year.": Claims the revenue is 5 times the prior year, but base 1.22 means 1.22 times the prior year (22% annual growth), not 5.
  • "When the company's predicted annual revenue is approximately $648,650$ dollars, it is $5\%$ greater than the predicted annual revenue for the previous year.": Claims 5% annual growth, but the base 1.22 indicates 22% annual growth.
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
